scope.summary: Introduce the missing `session` layer (ADR-0003): a SessionManager that owns N concurrent per-account zalo sessions (API + realtime Listener) keyed by account_id, with add/remove/restart lifecycle, graceful listener shutdown via Listener::stop(), and a per-account send throttle (ban-risk r1). The command layer is refactored to delegate to SessionManager instead of holding the in-command HashMap<AccountId, Arc<API>> + Vec<Listener> store; behaviour the UI sees is unchanged.
goals: session/: SessionManager holding AccountSession { api, listener } keyed by AccountId (tokio::sync::Mutex<HashMap>, no new dependency)
session/: lifecycle — add (insert + replace prior listener with graceful stop), remove (stop listener + drop), restart, get api, list account ids
session/: per-account send throttle (minimum interval between sends) so automation cannot look spam-like; throttle state lives with the session
command/: ListenerState replaced by managed SessionManager; login/qr/restore/file paths register via SessionManager; send_message/list_contacts/list_groups resolve the API through it
Graceful shutdown: replacing or removing a session stops its Listener (Listener::stop) instead of leaking sockets in an unbounded Vec
nonGoals: UI account switcher / per-account routing (already present in src/lib/session.svelte.ts)
Adding a concurrency crate such as dashmap (would need an ADR)
Reconnect/backoff policy beyond what zca-rust already does

acceptance.description: session/ SessionManager compiles, owns per-account sessions, and unit tests cover add/replace (old listener stopped), remove, and throttle gating. zca-rust types stay confined to the zalo layer.
command/ delegates to SessionManager (no in-command session HashMap or listener Vec); cargo build + clippy -D warnings clean.

Live: a real account logs in, registers in SessionManager, sends via the throttled send_text path, the manager-owned listener surfaces the marker tagged with the correct account id, and remove() stops the listener; sidecar captures only non-secret diagnostics. (two_accounts_live additionally proves concurrent cardinality once a second test credential is provisioned.)
